cases.input: We're exporting customers (email, name, DOB, postal code) and support_tickets (free-text agent notes) to an external churn-modeling vendor under GDPR. What must we do so the export isn't identifiable, and how do we honor later deletion requests?
Design a tamper-evident audit log for admin actions in our payments console: who changed a payout limit, when, from where. Regulators require 7-year immutable retention.
Our analytics team can't tell where fct_orders.total_amount comes from and half the columns have no owner. Build us a data dictionary and column-level lineage for the orders mart.
Add quality checkpoints between the bronze, silver, and gold stages of our ELT so a malformed silver table can't flow to gold. We want a hard stop, not a warning.
We're a 12-source retailer standing up a new data platform; GDPR is in scope and marketing needs same-day order data. Recommend an architecture, governance model, and quality-SLA approach.
Nobody owns our customer-360 dataset and disputes about field definitions never get resolved. Set up an ownership, stewardship, and escalation model.
Turn this quarter's retention metrics into a 3-slide narrative for the exec team: churn rose in the SMB segment but enterprise improved. Make it honest, not spin.
We need to 'get our data house in order' for an upcoming audit — there's something about who can see HR salary fields and who's accountable for them. Where do we start?
Write the dbt model SQL that joins stripe.charges to orders and divides amount by 100.
Anonymize our patients table for research before Friday.
